chief_complaint: A 70-year-old male presented with gradually worsening shortness of breath over two months.
history: The patient had a history of chronic obstructive pulmonary disease (COPD) and previously underwent endobronchial valve (EBV) removal due to pneumonia caused by long-term implantation of EBVs for bulla volume reduction after a left emphysematous bulla rupture and subsequent pneumothorax.
exam: Vital signs and physical examination findings were not provided.
labs: Chest radiography and computed tomography (CT) showed a giant emphysematous bulla (GEB) compressing 60% of the right lung volume, with the largest part filling the entire cross-section of the right lower lung.
